summary: Phase 9.A.7 (ARCHITECTURE.md polish + diagram per docs/PHASES.md:583 deliverable) approved. Documentation-only iteration. The pre-9.A.7 ARCHITECTURE.md was 1421 lines covering all 8 phases' frozen contracts but lacked: (a) a quick-navigation surface (a reader had to scroll to find a phase); (b) a 'how to read this doc' preamble distinguishing frozen contracts from background notes; (c) a system overview that reflected the Phase 8 router/share/verifier/save peer layers (the original ASCII diagram was Phase 1-vintage and stated 'Phase 1 builds core/ only. The remaining boxes are stubbed; they slot in as their phases land' — false post-Phase-8). Concrete deliverables: (1) New 'How to read this doc' preamble (lines 12-29) distinguishing the two flavors of content: 'Frozen contracts' (load-bearing byte-level definitions; pinned to the bit; changing requires architecture-red-team-reviewed rulesetVersion bump or a regression fix; sections beginning with 'Phase N frozen contracts' are entirely frozen-contract content) vs 'Background notes' (system overview, layer boundaries, lint inventory, runtime dependencies, testing strategy, operational concerns; documentation that evolves; updates do not require rulesetVersion bump). The decision rule: 'anything inside a fenced code block under a Phase N frozen contracts header is frozen; prose around it is descriptive.' (2) New 'Quick navigation' table-of-contents (lines 31-50) with anchor links to all 13 frozen-contract sections + 5 supporting sections (System overview, Layer boundaries, Build-time constants, Lint rule inventory, Runtime dependencies, Testing strategy, Operational concerns). (3) Updated system overview ASCII diagram (lines 52-93) — ADDS three Phase 8 sub-diagrams to the original Phase 1 diagram: (a) the 4 Phase 8 sharing peer layers (router / share / verifier / save) shown as peers that consume simulation outputs but do NOT feed sim — explicit anti-cycle annotation referencing the tests/build/rules-files-reachability.test.ts gate; (b) the Phase 5 + 7 + 9 user-visible UI layer (hud / inventory / equipment / win-screen / title-screen) with the 'all UI text routes through ui/theme/strings (Phase 9)' annotation per Phase 9.A.3 acceptance criterion 3; (c) the build-pipeline annotation (scripts/build-dual.mjs + publish-dual.mjs) per Phase 8.A.3. (4) Stale 'Phase 1 builds core/ only' tombstone REPLACED with a multi-paragraph explanation of the Phase 8 sharing-peer pattern + the anti-cycle defense surfaced by tests/build/rules-files-reachability.test.ts. The diagram now accurately reflects the post-Phase-8 + post-Phase-9.A architecture — a first-time reader can see at a glance the 13-layer organization. All sandbox gates green: 1194 tests passing across 83 test files (unchanged from Phase 9.A.6 — 9.A.7 is documentation-only); 0 lint errors; 0 typecheck errors; build dist/assets/index-*.js = 146.20 KB raw / 43.18 KB gzipped (unchanged from 9.A.6 — no production code added); CSS unchanged. Cross-runtime golden chain preserved unchanged (RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST + MAPGEN_DIGEST + SIM_DIGEST + ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed values + INVENTORY_DIGEST + WIN_DIGEST + REPLAY_DIGEST). The 1421-line ARCHITECTURE.md grew by ~70 lines to ~1491 lines (preamble + TOC + expanded diagram + Phase 8 anti-cycle annotation); no existing frozen-contract section was modified — the polish is purely additive and reorganizational.
suggested_commit_message: phase-9.A.7: ARCHITECTURE.md polish (How-to-read preamble + Quick-navigation TOC + Phase 8 sharing-peer layers added to system overview diagram + stale Phase 1 'in progress' tombstone replaced with anti-cycle annotation)

verification_sprint.purpose: Confirm that Phase 9.A.7's ARCHITECTURE.md polish (a) gives a first-time reader a quick-navigation surface for the 1421+ line document so they can jump to a specific phase's frozen contracts; (b) distinguishes frozen-contract content (load-bearing, requires architecture-red-team review to change) from background notes (incidental documentation that evolves freely); (c) updates the system overview diagram to reflect the post-Phase-8 + post-Phase-9.A architecture (4 sharing peer layers + 5 UI consumers + theme registry); (d) preserves every prior-phase invariant byte-for-byte (cross-runtime golden chain unchanged; existing test goldens preserved; lint/typecheck/test/build all green). The phase is documentation-only — no production-code changes — so the verification surface is the gates' baseline preservation.
verification_sprint.unit_tests: 1194 passed across 83 test files (unchanged from Phase 9.A.6 — 9.A.7 adds no test surface; the ARCHITECTURE.md polish is documentation). The cross-runtime golden chain (RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST + MAPGEN_DIGEST + SIM_DIGEST + ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed expectedHash values + INVENTORY_DIGEST + WIN_DIGEST + REPLAY_DIGEST) is preserved verbatim.
verification_sprint.lint: eslint . — 0 errors. Markdown files are not lint-scoped (eslint targets .ts/.js/.cjs only); no lint surface change.
verification_sprint.typecheck: tsc -b --noEmit — 0 errors. No TypeScript changes in 9.A.7.
verification_sprint.build: vite build — green. dist/assets/index-*.js = 146.20 KB raw / 43.18 KB gzipped (unchanged from Phase 9.A.6); CSS unchanged. Bundle remains well under the 110 KB CI gate.
verification_sprint.primary_workflow: No user-visible behavior change in 9.A.7 — the phase is documentation-only. The diagnostic page + playable game + title screen + CRT shader toggle from prior phases continue to behave identically. Cross-runtime e2e cannot run in-sandbox (Playwright browser binaries not preinstalled — established X.B sandbox limitation per docs/PHASES.md and the deploy.yml `npx playwright install --with-deps` step on the ubuntu-24.04 runner).

how_to_read_preamble_added.evidence: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md lines 12-29 (between the existing intro and the System overview section): new 'How to read this doc' preamble. Distinguishes 'Frozen contracts' (load-bearing byte-level definitions pinned to the bit; changing requires architecture-red-team-reviewed rulesetVersion bump or regression fix; sections beginning with 'Phase N frozen contracts' are entirely frozen-contract content) from 'Background notes' (system overview, layer boundaries, lint inventory, runtime dependencies, testing strategy, operational concerns; documentation that evolves; updates do not require rulesetVersion bump). Decision rule: 'anything inside a fenced code block under a Phase N frozen contracts header is frozen; prose around it is descriptive.' This addresses the long-standing ambiguity where a reader couldn't tell at a glance whether a given section was load-bearing or descriptive.

quick_navigation_toc_added.evidence: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md lines 31-50: new 'Quick navigation' section with anchor-link bullets to all major sections. Coverage: System overview + Layer boundaries (the 13-layer table); 13 individual frozen-contract anchors (Stream derivation / RunStreams.__consumed / Floor data model / Floor JSON / Stable-ID registries / seedToBytes / State-hash chain / Action descriptor encoding / Run fingerprint / 6 phase-specific frozen-contract sections covering Phases 3-8); 5 supporting sections (Build-time constants / Lint rule inventory / Runtime dependencies / Testing strategy / Operational concerns). A reader landing on the doc can now jump to any section in one click rather than scrolling 1421+ lines.

system_overview_diagram_updated.evidence: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md lines 52-93: the system overview ASCII diagram is extended from the original Phase 1 single-block layout to a 3-section diagram showing: (a) the original Phase 1-7 simulation pipeline (input → sim → render → canvas + sim → mapgen + core); (b) the Phase 8 sharing peer layers (router / share / verifier / save) shown as peers that consume simulation outputs but do NOT feed sim — explicit anti-cycle annotation referencing the tests/build/rules-files-reachability.test.ts gate; (c) the Phase 5 + 7 + 9 user-visible UI layer (hud / inventory / equipment / win-screen / title-screen) with the 'All UI text routes through ui/theme/strings (Phase 9)' annotation per Phase 9.A.3 acceptance criterion 3. The diagram now accurately reflects the post-Phase-8 + post-Phase-9.A architecture — a first-time reader can see at a glance the 13-layer organization without having to mentally synthesize across the full layer-boundary table.

stale_phase_1_tombstone_replaced.evidence: The pre-9.A.7 doc had this stale line: 'Phase 1 builds core/ only. The remaining boxes are stubbed; they slot in as their phases land.' This was Phase 1-vintage and false post-Phase-8 (every box is now real). 9.A.7 REPLACES it with a multi-paragraph explanation: 'Phase 8 added the four sharing peer layers (router, share, verifier, save) that consume simulation outputs but do NOT feed the simulation — they sit alongside sim/ rather than above or below it. The tests/build/rules-files-reachability.test.ts gate enforces the anti-cycle: nothing in router/, share/, verifier/, or save/ is reachable from src/sim/harness.ts's static-import graph.' This is correct + load-bearing documentation of the Phase 8 anti-cycle property.

frozen_contract_sections_unchanged.evidence: Phase 9.A.7's polish is purely ADDITIVE and reorganizational. No existing frozen-contract section was modified — every byte-level encoding (Stream derivation, RunStreams.__consumed, Floor data model + JSON schema, Stable-ID registries, seedToBytes, State-hash chain, Action descriptor encoding, Run fingerprint, Phase 3 entity model + turn loop + combat, Phase 4 atlas pipeline, Phase 5 renderer + input + ui, Phase 6 items + currency + equipment, Phase 7 NPCs + shops + boss, Phase 8 router + share + verifier + save) remains byte-for-byte unchanged. The cross-runtime golden chain (RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST + MAPGEN_DIGEST + SIM_DIGEST + ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed values + INVENTORY_DIGEST + WIN_DIGEST + REPLAY_DIGEST) continues to assert byte-identity through the existing self-test entries; no test golden moved.
frozen_contract_sections_unchanged.lines: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (Phase 1-8 frozen contract sections all preserved)

all_sandbox_gates_green.evidence: npm run lint: 0 errors. npm run typecheck: 0 errors. npm run test: 1194 passed across 83 test files (unchanged from Phase 9.A.6). npm run build: 146.20 KB raw / 43.18 KB gzipped (unchanged from 9.A.6 — 9.A.7 is documentation-only); CSS unchanged. E2e cannot run in-sandbox (established X.B limitation); the cross-runtime golden chain (incl. REPLAY_DIGEST entry from 8.A.2a) is exercised in Node — preserved unchanged.

phase_9_a_7_acceptance_complete.evidence: Phase 9.A.7 acceptance per docs/PHASES.md:583 deliverable + the prior 9.A.6 approval's next_phase scope (ARCHITECTURE.md polish + diagram): (1) ✅ Quick-navigation TOC at the top — anchor links to all 18+ sections; (2) ✅ How-to-read-this-doc preamble distinguishing frozen contracts from background notes; (3) ✅ System overview diagram updated to include Phase 8 sharing peer layers + Phase 9 UI consumers + theme registry annotation; (4) ✅ Stale Phase 1 'in progress' note tombstoned with the Phase 8 anti-cycle annotation; (5) ✅ All sandbox gates green; (6) ✅ Cross-runtime golden chain preserved unchanged. Note: a more visual Mermaid diagram would render natively on GitHub but the existing ASCII format was preserved for byte-stability + GitHub-render-agnosticism (the existing diagram was ASCII; switching to Mermaid would be a larger format change deferred to v1 release polish if a maintainer prefers it).

follow_ups_carried_forward: Phase 9.A.5 (LICENSE creation) remains BLOCKED on maintainer license-choice authorization. Both README.md (from 9.A.1) and CONTRIBUTING.md (from 9.A.6) note the pending state. Strategy options for the next iteration: (a) the maintainer resolves the license choice → next iteration creates LICENSE + updates package.json's `license` field; (b) the next iteration writes artifacts/phase-blocked.json with `blocking_question: 'Which license should the project ship under (MIT / Apache-2.0 / BSD-3-Clause / GPL-3 / other)?'`; (c) the agent loop continues to fall through to 9.A.8 (formal accessibility audit) and 9.B (live-deploy + Lighthouse), then writes phase-blocked at v1-release-prep when LICENSE is the last gating concern.
Phase 9 audio + itch.io + custom-domain + atlas-recipe-tuning items remain BLOCKED on external input. None are in Phase 9 acceptance criteria; v1 ships without them.
All Phase 8 + Phase 9.A.1-9.A.6 follow-ups continue to carry forward — they're polish targets, not gating.

Mermaid-format ARCHITECTURE.md diagram (per code-review feedback patterns from prior phases) — the current ASCII diagram is byte-stable and GitHub-renders correctly. A Mermaid version would render as an inline graphic on GitHub but is a larger format change. Deferred to v1 release polish if a maintainer prefers the visual upgrade.
follow_ups_observations: Phase 9.A.7 is documentation-only — no production code changes, no test changes, no bundle-size delta. The existing 1194 vitest tests pass unchanged; the build is byte-identical. The phase's value is in the contributor-onboarding surface: a first-time reader of ARCHITECTURE.md can now (a) see the 18-section quick-navigation TOC at the top and jump to any specific phase's frozen contracts in one click, (b) understand from the preamble whether a section is load-bearing (frozen contract) or descriptive (background notes), and (c) see the post-Phase-8 + post-Phase-9.A architecture in the system overview diagram (4 sharing peer layers + 5 UI consumers + theme registry annotation) without having to mentally synthesize across the 13-layer boundary table.

The polish is intentionally minimal-scope: preamble + TOC + diagram update + stale-tombstone replacement. The ~1300 lines of frozen-contract content (Phase 1-8 byte-level encodings) are preserved verbatim — no risk of accidental drift in a load-bearing surface from the doc-polish iteration.

Phase 9 progress: 9.A.1 (README + a11y CSS) → 9.A.2 (title screen) → 9.A.3 (theme registry) → 9.A.4 (CRT shader) → 9.A.5 (LICENSE — BLOCKED) → 9.A.6 (CONTRIBUTING.md) → 9.A.7 (ARCHITECTURE.md polish, this approval) → 9.A.8 (formal a11y audit) → 9.B (live-deploy + Lighthouse > 90). Out of the 8 sub-phases, 7 are complete or sandbox-resolvable; only 9.A.5 (LICENSE) is blocked. v1 release ships after 9.B; project-complete.json is written at that point. Audio + itch.io + custom-domain + atlas-tuning remain deferred to v1.1 polish per the 9.A.1 split rationale.

The next iteration should pick up phase-9.A.8 (formal accessibility audit per docs/PHASES.md:586). The current accessibility surface (from 9.A.1's prefers-reduced-motion + :focus-visible CSS gates and 9.A.2's keyboard-only Tab/Enter contract for the title screen) is solid but unaudited against WCAG. The audit should verify: (a) all interactive elements are keyboard-reachable in a sensible Tab order; (b) color contrast meets WCAG AA (4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text + UI components); (c) the prefers-reduced-motion gate is exhaustive (no missed animations); (d) the focus-ring is visible on every focusable element; (e) ARIA labels are present where automated tools can't infer the role (e.g., the canvas + the CRT toggle button benefit from aria-label clarification); (f) the diagnostic-page + playable-game flows are completable via screen-reader narration. Phase 9.A.8 may surface CSS / DOM updates and a small set of new a11y-specific e2e tests.
next_phase: Phase 9.A.8 — formal accessibility audit per docs/PHASES.md:586 deliverable ('Accessibility pass: keyboard-only navigation, contrast check, prefers-reduced-motion respected'). The current accessibility surface (from 9.A.1's prefers-reduced-motion + :focus-visible gates and 9.A.2's keyboard-only Tab/Enter contract) is solid but unaudited against WCAG AA. Phase 9.A.8 should: (a) verify keyboard-only Tab order on the title screen + diagnostic page + playable game (Tab cycles through every interactive element in a sensible order; no focus-traps; no inaccessible buttons); (b) check color contrast for the cyberpunk neon palette against WCAG AA (4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text + UI components — the current --fg #d6deeb on --bg #0c0e14 is ~13.7:1 which exceeds AA; the --muted #7080a0 on --bg is ~5.5:1; the --green #6fe3a8 on --bg is ~10.4:1; the --accent #c792ea on --bg is ~7.4:1 — all pass AA, but the rgba alpha-blended button backgrounds need verification); (c) confirm the prefers-reduced-motion gate from 9.A.1 is exhaustive (currently the page has no animations to throttle but the CRT shader from 9.A.4 + future polish need verification); (d) audit ARIA labels on the canvas (currently tabindex=0 with no aria-label — should have role='img' + aria-label='Game canvas' or role='application' + clarifying aria-label; same for the CRT shader toggle which currently has only a label-text — adding aria-pressed='true|false' would improve screen-reader semantics); (e) add a small set of a11y-specific e2e tests using @axe-core/playwright if available, or hand-rolled keyboard-only navigation tests. After 9.A.8: 9.B (live-deploy + Lighthouse > 90 verification per acceptance criterion 2). v1 release ships after 9.B; project-complete.json is written at that point. Phase 9.A.5 (LICENSE) remains BLOCKED on maintainer license-choice authorization; if the choice is not resolved by the v1-release-prep iteration, that iteration should write artifacts/phase-blocked.json with a license-choice question.
